specification.topic-statement: The wait tool handler translates a JSON tool invocation from the LLM into a blocking wait operation and serializes the result back into a JSON envelope for the tool call infrastructure.

scope.in-scope: Argument parsing and validation for the wait tool, dispatch to the appropriate wait mode based on argument presence, and construction of the JSON result object from the wait result structure.
scope.boundary: The blocking poll loop, PostgreSQL notification registration, mailbox queries, fan-in state tracking, and interrupt handling are implemented by the wait synchronization subsystem. This topic invokes that subsystem but does not implement it.
The raw JSON result produced here is wrapped in a standard tool envelope before being returned to the tool execution infrastructure.

field.name: timeout
field.note: required; maximum seconds to wait; 0 means non-blocking instant check

field.name: from_agents
field.note: optional; if present and is an array, enables fan-in mode; each element must be a string UUID

field.name: from
field.note: present only when a message was received; UUID of the sending agent

field.name: message
field.note: present only when a message was received; the message body

field.name: status
field.note: present only when no message was received; the value 'timeout'

field.name: results
field.note: one object per target agent, in the order targets were specified

field.name: results[].agent_name
field.note: display name of the target agent

field.name: results[].status
field.note: one of: running, received, idle, dead

field.name: results[].message
field.note: present only when status is 'received'; the message body

behavior.argument-parsing:
The JSON argument string is parsed. If parsing fails, the handler returns an error with code PARSE_ERROR and does not proceed.
The parsed object must contain a "timeout" field whose value is any numeric type. If the field is absent or not a number, the handler returns an error with code INVALID_ARG. The timeout value is truncated to a signed 32-bit integer.
The presence of a "from_agents" field is then examined. Fan-in mode is activated if and only if the field is present in the parsed object and its value is a JSON array. A missing field or a field with a non-array value (including null) selects next-message mode. An empty array is treated as a valid fan-in invocation with zero targets.
Each element of the "from_agents" array is validated to be a string during the element-copying loop. If a non-string element is encountered, the handler returns an error with code INVALID_ARG at that point, but elements already copied into the target array before the failing element are left allocated.
behavior.next-message-dispatch:
When fan-in mode is not selected, the handler delegates to the next-message wait operation, passing the calling agent's identifier, the session identifier, the timeout, and the agent's interrupt flag.
The wait runs synchronously on the calling thread (which is the tool execution background thread). The main REPL thread is not involved until the wait completes.
behavior.fan-in-dispatch:
When fan-in mode is selected, all UUIDs from the "from_agents" array are copied into a fresh array. The handler then delegates to the fan-in wait operation, passing the calling agent's identifier, the session identifier, the timeout, the UUID array, the count of targets, and the agent's interrupt flag.
The wait runs synchronously on the calling thread.
behavior.result-serialization:
After the wait operation returns, the handler constructs a JSON result object from the wait result structure:
For next-message mode: - If a message was received (indicated by a non-null sender identifier), the result object contains a "from" field with the sender UUID and a "message" field with the message body. - If no message was received (timeout or interrupt), the result object contains a "status" field with the value "timeout".
When the wait is interrupted (e.g., due to a user abort), the result object is constructed the same way as a timeout: a "status" field with value "timeout". The caller cannot distinguish an interrupt from a genuine timeout expiration through the JSON result alone.
For fan-in mode: - A "results" array is constructed with one object per entry in the wait result. Each object contains "agent_uuid", "agent_name", and "status" fields. A "message" field is added only when the entry's message is non-null.
The JSON result is wrapped in a success envelope and returned.
